name: Ascent to Pico del Lobo via Collado del Aventadero — circular from Pinilla
name_original: Subida al Pico del Lobo por el Collado del Aventadero — circular desde Pinilla
region_intro: Jogging around Cerezo De Arriba offers diverse terrain at the foot of the Ayllón mountain range, providing a varied landscape for runners. The region features significant elevation changes, including the high peak of Pico del Lobo, alongside more gentle paths along the Cerezuelo River. Extensive pine forests like El Pinar del Raso and ancient transhumance routes such as the Cañada Real Soriana Occidental provide a network of trails. This natural setting combines mountainous challenges with scenic forest and riverside running opportunities. Best jogging routes around Cerezo De Arriba The most popular jogging route is Ascent to Pico del Lobo via Collado del Aventadero — circular from Pinilla, a 7.8 miles (12.6 km) trail that takes 2 hours 23 minutes to complete. This difficult route offers a challenging ascent to a high peak with panoramic views. Another top favourite among local runners is Pico del Lobo (2,274 m) – Pico del Lobo loop from La Pinilla Estación de Esquí, a difficult 8.0 miles (12.9 km) path. This loop starts from the La Pinilla ski resort, leading to the high elevations of Pico del Lobo. Local runners also love the Pinilla – Pico del Lobo (2,274 m) loop from Pradera de San Benito, a 26.3 miles (42.3 km) trail leading through mountainous terrain, often completed in about 7 hours 14 minutes. Jogging around Cerezo De Arriba is defined by the Ayllón mountain range, the Cerezuelo River's riparian forests, and extensive pine groves. The network offers options for different ability levels, from challenging mountain ascents to more moderate forest paths. The routes in Cerezo De Arriba are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 4 reviews. More than 400 runners have used komoot to explore Cerezo De Arriba's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on running trails around Cerezo De Arriba?
A: Jogging around Cerezo De Arriba offers a diverse landscape, from the challenging mountainous terrain of the Ayllón mountain range, including the high peak of Pico del Lobo, to more gentle paths along the Cerezuelo River. You'll also find extensive pine forests like El Pinar del Raso and ancient transhumance routes such as the Cañada Real Soriana Occidental, providing a varied network of trails.

Q: Are there running routes suitable for beginners in Cerezo De Arriba?
A: Yes, while many routes in the area are challenging, there are 2 easy running routes available. These are perfect for those new to running or looking for a more relaxed experience amidst the natural beauty of Cerezo De Arriba.

Q: Are there family-friendly jogging routes in Cerezo De Arriba?
A: For families, the gentler paths along the Cerezuelo River or within El Pinar del Raso offer pleasant environments for a jog or walk. These areas provide abundant vegetation and a more relaxed setting compared to the mountainous trails. Look for routes categorized as 'easy' or 'moderate' for suitable options.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the running trails in Cerezo De Arriba?
A: Many natural trails in the Cerezo De Arriba region, especially those through forests and along rivers, are generally suitable for dogs. However, it's always recommended to keep your dog on a leash, especially in areas with wildlife or near livestock, and to check local regulations for specific trails or protected areas.

Q: What natural features or landmarks can I see while jogging in Cerezo De Arriba?
A: While running, you can encounter several notable natural features. The majestic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2176894" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico del Lobo&lt;/a&gt; offers impressive views, and the lush riparian forests along the Cerezuelo River provide scenic beauty. You might also discover the tranquil &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2322047" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Chorrera de los Litueros Waterfall&lt;/a&gt; or explore the serene &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5437316" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Birch Forest of Somosierra&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there circular running routes available in Cerezo De Arriba?
A: Yes, there are several circular running routes. For a challenging experience, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1164615523/ascent-to-pico-del-lobo-via-collado-del-aventadero-circular-from-pinilla" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Pico del Lobo via Collado del Aventadero — circular from Pinilla&lt;/a&gt;, which is 7.8 miles (12.6 km) long. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/20143157" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Riofrío Reservoir – Pico del Lobo loop from Riofrío de Riaza&lt;/a&gt;, covering 14.3 miles (23.1 km).

Q: What is the best time of year for running in Cerezo De Arriba?
A: The region is excellent for running during spring and autumn when the weather is milder and the natural scenery is vibrant. Autumn, in particular, is beautiful with the changing colors of the forests like El Pinar del Raso. While summer offers longer daylight hours, higher elevations can provide cooler temperatures. Winter running is possible, especially around La Pinilla ski resort, but requires appropriate gear for snow and colder conditions.

Q: Are there any running routes that offer views of the Ayllón mountain range?
A: Absolutely. Many routes, especially those leading towards Pico del Lobo, provide stunning panoramic views of the Ayllón mountain range. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/24541014" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pico del Lobo (2,274 m) – Aventadero Pass loop from La Pinilla Estación de Esquí&lt;/a&gt; offers extensive mountain vistas throughout its 11.8 miles (19 km) length.

Q: How many running routes are available in Cerezo De Arriba?
A: There are 37 running routes available around Cerezo De Arriba. These routes cater to various fitness levels, with 2 easy, 8 moderate, and 27 difficult options, ensuring there's a trail for every runner.

Q: What do other runners enjoy the most about jogging in Cerezo De Arriba?
A: The running routes in Cerezo De Arriba are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 4 reviews. Runners often praise the diverse terrain, from challenging mountain ascents to scenic forest and riverside paths, and the impressive views of the Ayllón mountain range.

Q: Are there any long-distance running options in the area?
A: Yes, for those seeking longer runs, the ancient Cañada Real Soriana Occidental crosses through Cerezo De Arriba. This established path offers extensive routes suitable for long-distance jogging. Additionally, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/39837794" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Pinilla – Pico del Lobo (2,274 m) loop from Pradera de San Benito&lt;/a&gt; is a challenging 26.3 miles (42.3 km) trail for experienced runners.
tips: The route to Collado del Aventadero is popular with hikers and mountaineers due to its stunning panoramic views and the diversity of landscapes that can be seen along the way. The trail passes through pine forests and offers spectacular views of the surrounding mountains.

At the top of Pico del Lobo is the geodesic vertex, which has extraordinary views, and the remains of an old gondola building with a deplorable and embarrassing appearance, it is incredible that these remains of industrial waste still remain in the mountains, especially when They are protected natural spaces. One solution could be to reuse the materials and build a small mountain refuge. The descent begins going down from the summit at the same place, surrounding the skeleton of rubble, and immediately heads west towards the cliffs of Cuerda de la Pinilla. In this sector of the mountains, the trail is dug into the crest of the cord and with enough snow it could disappear. The rest of the year is a fantastic section with the entire abyss of the Majada de los Carneros on the left side.

The trail leaves the car park where the Campo Base forest park entrance is in the direction of Riaza, leaving the access to the ski resort on the right. The entrance to the La Pinilla apartment complex appears immediately. The route follows the little road that goes up towards the buildings, goes around a line of apartments to the left and, leaving the curve, takes a detour that enters the pine forest. The road has a barrier so that cars do not enter. In the urbanization there are several options and we have chosen the most direct ascent. The wall at the beginning impresses so quickly, with almost no time to warm up your legs, but it takes a few minutes to walk until it connects with an old forest track that also goes up from the buildings and heads towards the peaks. The path passes the course of the Serrano river, next to a water intake, and undertakes a long climb up the slopes of the sierra.
